sections.question: Whether a member of the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board is in conflict of interest for reviewing the decision of a hearing examiner who decided an application for increase of rent in Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park where the member is a tenant. Specifically: (1) If Ms. Coe considers an issue that concerns all five mobilehome parks in Rohnert Park, is she precluded from participating? (2) If Ms. Coe considers an issue affecting some, but not all mobilehome parks where Rancho Verde is not involved, does such participation constitute a conflict? (3) Same as (2) but where Rancho Verde is one of the affected parks. (4) Is Ms. Coe precluded from participating in any decision that affects Rancho Verde only? (5) If not, under what circumstances is she precluded? (6) If the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board elects to hear a rent increase application, is Ms. Coe precluded from hearing such an application regardless of which park it originates from? (7) What conflict of interest constraints would apply to a mobilehome park owner appointed to the Rent Review Board relative to his or her park, and do different standards apply compared to tenants?

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Rent Review Board member who is a tenant in a specific mobilehome park (Rancho Verde) has a conflict of interest when reviewing rent decisions involving that park, and under what circumstances such conflicts arise for similar situations and for mobilehome park owners on the Board.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains detailed factual background about a rent review proceeding and multiple specific questions regarding conflict of interest applicability. The letter references a prior opinion issued by the city attorney but requests FPPC guidance. Document is from 1992 and uses older formatting conventions.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a member of the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board is in conflict of interest for reviewing the decision of a hearing examiner who decided an application for increase of rent in Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park where the member is a tenant. Specifically: (1) If Ms. Coe considers an issue that concerns all five mobilehome parks in Rohnert Park, is she precluded from participating? (2) If Ms. Coe considers an issue affecting some, but not all mobilehome parks where Rancho Verde is not involved, does such participation constitute a conflict? (3) Same as (2) but where Rancho Verde is one of the affected parks. (4) Is Ms. Coe precluded from participating in any decision that affects Rancho Verde only? (5) If not, under what circumstances is she precluded? (6) If the Rohnert Park Rent Review Board elects to hear a rent increase application, is Ms. Coe precluded from hearing such an application regardless of which park it originates from? (7) What conflict of interest constraints would apply to a mobilehome park owner appointed to the Rent Review Board relative to his or her park, and do different standards apply compared to tenants?

embedding.summary: This is a request from the Rohnert Park City Attorney to the FPPC for an opinion on whether a Rent Review Board member (Ms. Coe) who is a tenant at Rancho Verde Mobilehome Park has a conflict of interest in reviewing a rent increase decision for that park, with detailed questions about the scope and applicability of conflict restrictions in various scenarios.
